product: A15 Wide + A15 Focus (L-Acoustics A15, medium-throw line source; Wide and Focus are coverage variants of the same enclosure)

electroacoustic.frequency_range_hz: Focus: 41-20000; Wide: 42-20000 (usable bandwidth, -10 dB, [A15] preset); extends to 29 Hz when paired with KS21
electroacoustic.max_spl_note: A15 Focus 144 dB and A15 Wide 141 dB (peak at 1 m, free field, pink noise crest factor 4, [A15] preset)
electroacoustic.directivity: Enclosure (WST constant curvature): Wide 30 deg, Focus 10 deg (-6 dB). Horizontal directivity is Panflex/L-Fins selectable: 70 deg or 110 deg symmetric, or 90 deg asymmetric.
electroacoustic.components: LF: 1 x 15-inch neodymium cone driver (bass-reflex, L-Vents). HF: 1 x 3-inch diaphragm neodymium compression driver on DOSC waveguide with Panflex (L-Fins). 2-way passive design.

operating_modes: low latency (for [A15_MO] monitor preset and compatible sub channel sets)
amplification.per_amplifier_note: Manual drive-capacity table lists LA4X at 1 enclosure per output / 4 total and LA12X at 3 per output / 12 total. Same [A15] preset drives all Wide/Focus directivity settings.

connection.input_connector: 2x 4-point speakON (NL4), used interchangeably as IN or LINK
connection.internal_pinout: speakON 1+/1- carry the passive enclosure signal; 2+/2- not linked
connection.breakouts: SP-Y1 breakout (4-point speakON to 2 x 2-point speakON, with CC4FP adapter) for two passive enclosures; DOSUB-LA8 (8-point PA-COM to 4 x 2-point speakON) for four passive enclosures

physical.weight_note: A15 Wide 33 kg / 73 lb; A15 Focus 35 kg / 77 lb (net)
physical.cabinet: Premium grade Baltic beech and birch plywood, coated steel grille with acoustically neutral 3D fabric, dark grey brown Pantone 426 C finish; rigging components in high-grade steel with anti-corrosion coating

deployment.max_per_flown_array: Vertical array on A15-BUMP (+ optional M-BAR): max 8; on A15-RIGBAR: max 4. Radial array: up to 9 with 3x A15-LIFT. Safe limits are lower and configuration-dependent (verify in Soundvision).
deployment.inter_element_angles: Splay angles are configuration-dependent and set via the A15-BUMP/A15-LIFT hole patterns; determine exact values in Soundvision rather than from a fixed list
rigging_accessories: A-TILT (stack onto KS21 with site-angle adjustment)
KS21-OUTRIG (stability bars, mandatory when stacking A15 on KS21)
A-MOUNT (35 mm / 1-3/8 in pole mount, single enclosure)

usage: Medium-throw line source intended for production and concert work; flown or stacked in mixed Wide/Focus columns where Focus (10 deg) handles longer projection (up to ~45 m) and Wide (30 deg) fills broad near-to-mid coverage.

Each enclosure is a single passive 8-ohm box needing one amplifier channel; budget output count by amp: up to 12 on an LA12X (3 per output), up to 10 on an LA7.16i, and 4 on an LA4X or LA2Xi (SE), dropping to 2 on LA2Xi in bridged mode.

Pair with KS21 subwoofers (recommended roughly 1 sub per enclosure) to reinforce the low-frequency contour and extend the system bandwidth down to about 29 Hz; remember to apply the published pre-alignment delay between the A15 and sub presets.
Use Panflex to match horizontal coverage to the room: pick 70 deg or 110 deg symmetric, or 90 deg asymmetric, all driven by the same [A15] preset so no re-tuning is needed when changing fins.

Flexible single-box roles: X-BAR for a flown point source, A-MOUNT for pole-mounted delay/fill, or [A15_MO] (with low-latency option) for stage monitors; a custom preset is required if a monitor is combined with a sub.

Respect mechanical limits before flying: max 8 per A15-BUMP vertical array (4 on A15-RIGBAR) and use KS21-OUTRIG whenever A15 are stacked on KS21; always confirm safe element counts and splay angles in Soundvision.
